text:
Anchora sailed across the vast blue sea. She loved animals dearly. One afternoon a baby sea lion stuck its head out from a sea lion puppery.

Anchora saved the baby. Every sail she flew made her think of the little creature. One grey sock fell out. She knit another. Life sailed on.

They sailed west across the Atlantic till they found a desert. The sun was hot. A big old thirsty burrowing owl begged Anchora to take him south.

Anchora did.

She took the owl a lot of sun-crickets.

They flew together. The owl's burrows filled with berries.

And birds. And bugs. And more berries.

So they flew and flew till they were old birds. They loved each other.

Then they flew south.


That's all.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
